text:
Once upon an autumn evening, young Lily sat on a hill overlooking her sleepy village. The leaves glowed gold and rust around her, casting warm shadows. She glanced at the fireflies flickering like tiny lanterns in the twilight.

A gentle breeze carried the scent of fallen leaves and freshly baked bread from the village bakery. Lily closed her eyes and sighed contentedly, wishing she could wander these woods all day. Her imagination danced with every rustle in the brush and every gentle swaying of the willows.

In a clearing by the hillside, a figure emerged. It was Oliver, her neighbor and her best friend. Oliver had hair like silver moonlight and eyes as deep as the midnight sky.

“Got a moment?” Oliver asked, pausing beside her.

Lily nodded, amused. “Just watching the world go by.”

“The world’s a book, and those who do not read it—only imagine it.” Oliver grinned. “Want to hear a story?”
